title: Sweep: opt-in flag to capture universal-prototype members
description: legacy recursive captures inherited Object/Function/Array.prototype members (toString, valueOf, hasOwnProperty...) via its full-chain walk. The legacy->stealth sweep (openwpm/utilities/stealth_settings.py) currently routes these to residue by default (deliberate: wrapping Object.prototype.toString fires the wrapper on every object page-wide for zero tracking signal). Add a researcher opt-in flag (legacy_settings_to_stealth(capture_universal_prototype_members=False) + CLI --capture-universal-members) that, when on, captures them via the same B-prime interface-filtered shared-prototype path (object=Object/Function/Array, receiverInterfaces=reached interfaces) for strict legacy parity. Default off = unchanged (residue). Non-method universal members stay residue regardless. Worker a4f394484edaf173b in flight; lands as a new commit on feat/stealth-js-instrument-v2 (#1154). User decision: this is a researcher knob (give them rope to wrap every toString). the-hater pass after.
